evidence_quotes.quote: Every game purchase should guarantee permanent access.

paragraphs.text: This legislation must address a fundamental consumer rights violation: when we pay for a game, we should own it permanently, and it should continue working regardless of what the publisher decides. The principle is simple: if I pay for it, I own it. When I buy a physical book, the publisher can't come to my house and take it back when they stop printing it. When I buy a car, the manufacturer can't remotely disable it when they discontinue the model. Yet the gaming industry has somehow convinced regulators that digital purchases work differentlythey don't. Every game purchase should guarantee permanent access. If a publisher wants to shut down their servers, they must either: - Release the server software so communities can host it themselves - Patch the game to work offline or peer-to-peer - Provide a full refund for every copy sold
